title: Native SSH access with OIDC-backed short-lived SSH certificates for Ananta terminal targets

summary.short: Add a native SSH access path where OIDC authenticates the user and a trusted SSH CA issues short-lived SSH user certificates. SSH then enters an Ananta-controlled terminal wrapper which can start or attach to tmux sessions only after policy approval.
summary.problem: The existing tmux/OIDC terminal TODO covers browser and HTTPS/WSS terminal access, but not native SSH clients. Classic SSH does not provide OIDC as its normal protocol-level login model, so the clean production pattern is OIDC plus short-lived SSH certificates.
summary.goal: Introduce native SSH as a separate governed access mode: OIDC login produces a short-lived SSH certificate, OpenSSH validates that certificate, and the server-side Ananta wrapper starts controlled tmux only after TerminalPolicyService approval.
summary.non_goal: Do not make direct SSH shell access the default production path. Do not expose raw tmux sockets. Do not let SSH certificate possession skip Ananta user, target, workspace, goal/task or terminal policy checks.

access_modes.name: web_terminal_oidc_wss
access_modes.summary: Browser path from the existing tmux TODO: OIDC login, Ananta auth context, scoped attach token, HTTPS/WSS terminal stream.

access_modes.name: native_ssh_oidc_certificate
access_modes.summary: Native SSH path: OIDC login through a CLI/helper, short-lived SSH user certificate, OpenSSH certificate validation, Ananta terminal wrapper, tmux backend.

access_modes.name: local_dev_tmux
access_modes.summary: Local development path where tmux is available locally and remote access is disabled or not required.

flow: User runs ananta ssh login or equivalent helper.
Helper starts OIDC Authorization Code Flow with PKCE against the configured identity provider.
Ananta SSH certificate issuer validates the OIDC token and maps claims to internal Ananta identity and terminal permissions.
Issuer creates a short-lived SSH user certificate with constrained principals, extensions and validity.
User connects with normal OpenSSH using the certificate.
sshd validates the certificate through TrustedUserCAKeys and principal mapping.
A server-side Ananta terminal wrapper calls TerminalPolicyService before starting or attaching to tmux.
tmux starts only inside the allowed target, workspace and session scope.

components.name: SshCertificateIssuer
components.summary: Service that issues short-lived SSH user certificates after successful OIDC authentication and policy pre-checks.
responsibilities: Validate OIDC issuer, audience, expiry, nonce and signature.
Map OIDC subject, groups and roles to Ananta user identity.
Issue SSH certificates with short validity and constrained principals.
Add non-secret metadata such as user id, auth source, policy version and decision id where suitable.
Emit audit events for certificate issuance and denied issuance.

components.name: SshPrincipalMapper
components.summary: Deterministic mapper between Ananta identities/policies and OpenSSH principals.
responsibilities: Map user and role claims to allowed SSH principals.
Differentiate worker, hub and hub_as_worker access principals.
Reject unknown groups and unmapped roles by default.
Provide stable reason codes for denied mappings.

components.name: AnantaSshTerminalWrapper
components.summary: Server-side wrapper used by sshd ForceCommand or equivalent configuration to route SSH sessions into controlled Ananta terminal sessions.
responsibilities: Read validated SSH certificate identity from sshd-provided metadata.
Resolve target type, target id, workspace, goal id and task id from allowed CLI arguments or server-side defaults.
Call TerminalPolicyService before creating or attaching to a tmux session.
Deny Hub and Hub-as-Worker access unless explicit terminal.hub.* or terminal.hub_as_worker.* permissions exist.
Start tmux only through TmuxSessionBackend or a strict wrapper, never by exposing raw tmux sockets.

components.name: SshAccessAuditService
components.summary: Audit layer for SSH certificate issuance and SSH terminal attach/write/kill events.
responsibilities: Correlate certificate issuance, SSH login, terminal session creation and tmux lifecycle events.
Record certificate key id, principal, user id, target type, target id, decision id and policy version.
Avoid storing private certificate material, bearer tokens, OIDC tokens or raw secrets.
Support optional terminal recording policy for high-risk Hub sessions.

recommended_backends.name: smallstep_step_ca
recommended_backends.summary: Preferred open-source friendly option for OIDC-backed SSH CA workflows and short-lived SSH certificates.

recommended_backends.name: teleport
recommended_backends.summary: More complete access platform with SSO, RBAC and session recording. Useful as a heavier optional integration.

recommended_backends.name: ananta_internal_ssh_ca_later
recommended_backends.summary: Possible later built-in issuer, but only after policy, audit, CA key storage and operational controls are mature.

new_permissions.name: terminal.ssh.login
new_permissions.summary: Allow requesting a native SSH terminal login flow. Does not imply worker, hub or write permission.

new_permissions.name: terminal.ssh.certificate.issue
new_permissions.summary: Allow issuing a short-lived SSH user certificate for the mapped Ananta identity.

new_permissions.name: terminal.ssh.worker.create
new_permissions.summary: Allow creating worker terminal sessions through native SSH.

new_permissions.name: terminal.ssh.worker.attach
new_permissions.summary: Allow attaching to worker terminal sessions through native SSH.

new_permissions.name: terminal.ssh.hub.create
new_permissions.summary: Allow creating direct Hub terminal sessions through native SSH. High risk and denied by default.

new_permissions.name: terminal.ssh.hub.attach
new_permissions.summary: Allow attaching to direct Hub terminal sessions through native SSH. High risk and denied by default.

new_permissions.name: terminal.ssh.hub_as_worker.create
new_permissions.summary: Allow creating Hub-as-Worker terminal sessions through native SSH. Must be separate from worker SSH access.

new_permissions.name: terminal.ssh.hub_as_worker.attach
new_permissions.summary: Allow attaching to Hub-as-Worker terminal sessions through native SSH.

certificate_constraints.allowed_principal_pattern: deterministic allow-list only; no broad principal in production

implementation_tasks.title: Document native SSH as separate access mode
implementation_tasks.summary: Extend terminal documentation to distinguish web OIDC/WSS, native SSH with OIDC-backed certificates, and local dev tmux.
acceptance_criteria: Docs state that tmux itself has no OIDC support and must be protected by Ananta Gateway or SSH certificate flow.
Docs show web terminal and native SSH terminal as separate flows.
Docs explicitly reject raw tmux socket exposure.
Docs explain why production SSH should use short-lived certificates instead of long-lived shared credentials.

implementation_tasks.title: Select and model SSH CA backend integration
implementation_tasks.summary: Define whether the first implementation uses smallstep step-ca, Teleport, or an Ananta-managed abstraction with pluggable backend.
acceptance_criteria: ADR compares smallstep step-ca, Teleport and a later internal SSH CA option.
The selected first backend supports short-lived SSH certificates and OIDC identity binding.
CA signing material is isolated from workers, LLMs and normal terminal sessions.
Configuration supports disabling native SSH completely.

implementation_tasks.title: Add SSH certificate issuer service abstraction
implementation_tasks.summary: Introduce an issuer interface that validates OIDC identity and requests or creates constrained SSH user certificates.
acceptance_criteria: Issuer refuses invalid issuer, audience, expiry, nonce or signature.
Issuer refuses unknown OIDC groups by default.
Issued certificates have bounded validity and deterministic key id metadata.
Hub-target certificates use shorter maximum lifetime than worker-target certificates.
Tests prove that certificate issuance does not imply terminal attach/write authorization.

implementation_tasks.title: Add CLI flow for OIDC-backed SSH login
implementation_tasks.summary: Add commands that perform OIDC login, store short-lived SSH certificate material safely, and print or execute the ssh command.
acceptance_criteria: ananta ssh login starts OIDC Authorization Code Flow with PKCE or calls the configured backend helper.
ananta ssh targets lists only targets allowed by TerminalPolicyService.
ananta ssh connect refuses ambiguous target selection.
ananta ssh connect --target-type hub requires explicit reason and separate permission.
CLI does not print OIDC tokens or private certificate material.

implementation_tasks.title: Add server-side SSH terminal wrapper
implementation_tasks.summary: Create a strict wrapper that receives a validated SSH identity and opens only approved Ananta terminal sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Wrapper denies unmanaged direct shell mode for managed production targets unless explicitly configured.
Wrapper calls TerminalPolicyService before create, attach, read, write or kill operations.
Wrapper cannot turn worker SSH permission into Hub terminal access.
Wrapper validates workspace path and refuses path traversal or unconfigured host mount escape.
Wrapper starts tmux only through the safe tmux backend or equivalent controlled command path.

implementation_tasks.title: Add hardened OpenSSH configuration examples
implementation_tasks.summary: Document safe sshd_config patterns for TrustedUserCAKeys, principal mapping and ForceCommand integration.
acceptance_criteria: Example uses TrustedUserCAKeys for the configured SSH CA public key.
Example uses deterministic principal mapping or AuthorizedPrincipalsCommand.
Example uses ForceCommand or equivalent wrapper for managed terminal accounts.
Example disables unsafe credential fallback for production profile.
Example does not allow unmanaged direct shell mode for managed terminal users.

implementation_tasks.title: Add SSH audit model
implementation_tasks.summary: Ensure native SSH access is correlated with certificate issuance, terminal session lifecycle and policy decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Certificate issuance emits audit event with key id, user id, auth source, principal, decision id and expiry.
SSH attach/create/write/kill events are correlated to the certificate issuance event where possible.
Audit logs never store OIDC tokens, private certificate material or raw bearer tokens.
Deny-list strategy is documented for the selected backend.
Expired certificates cannot be used to create new terminal sessions.

implementation_tasks.title: Add native SSH security tests
implementation_tasks.summary: Test failure modes around certificates, principals, terminal wrapper, Hub access and stale credentials.
acceptance_criteria: Expired SSH certificate is rejected.
Certificate with unknown principal is rejected.
Broad principal is rejected in production profile.
SSH worker permission cannot attach to Hub session.
Unmanaged shell request is denied for managed targets.
Certificate issuance fails closed when policy service is unavailable.

global_acceptance_criteria: Native SSH access is unavailable unless explicitly enabled.
Native SSH access uses short-lived SSH certificates as the recommended production path.
OIDC identity is bound to every issued SSH certificate.
Certificate possession alone does not skip TerminalPolicyService.
Worker SSH access does not grant Hub or Hub-as-Worker terminal access.
Hub native SSH terminal access is denied by default and has shorter default certificate lifetime.
OpenSSH configuration examples use TrustedUserCAKeys and a forced Ananta wrapper for managed targets.
CA signing material is isolated from workers, LLMs, terminal sessions and frontend clients.
All SSH certificate issuance and SSH terminal lifecycle events are auditable.
The existing HTTPS/WSS OIDC terminal path remains separate from the native SSH certificate path.
open_questions: Should the first implementation require smallstep step-ca, or should Ananta define only an abstraction first?
Should native SSH ever allow unmanaged direct shell mode, or always force Ananta terminal wrapper for managed targets?
Should Hub SSH terminal access require interactive approval in addition to explicit policy permission?
Should SSH certificates be scoped to one target/session, or may they be scoped to a short-lived set of allowed targets?
